comment: Duplicate record_id: household-wave record identifiers must be unique across the file.
Duplicate household_id + wave combination: each household may appear at most once per wave. Split-offs receive new household_id values, so duplicates indicate a bad extract.

condition: record_id IS NOT NULL AND household_id IS NOT NULL AND wave BETWEEN 1 AND 20 AND record_id <> household_id || '_W' || lpad(CAST(wave AS VARCHAR), 2, '0')
comment: record_id does not decompose into household_id + '_W' + zero-padded wave; internal key components disagree.

condition: panel_entry_wave BETWEEN 1 AND 20 AND wave BETWEEN 1 AND 20 AND baseline_record IN (0, 1) AND (panel_entry_wave > wave OR (baseline_record = 1) <> (wave = panel_entry_wave))
comment: Panel entry logic inconsistent: panel_entry_wave must be <= wave and baseline_record = 1 exactly when wave = panel_entry_wave.
Household identifier must be HH followed by eight digits.
Core identifier fields must never be missing in any wave.
Distinct wave count outside 1-20 suggests a truncated or wrongly merged extract.

condition: interview_date IS NOT NULL AND TRY_CAST(interview_date AS DATE) IS NULL
comment: interview_date is not a real calendar date (e.g. Feb 30). The JSON Schema regex cannot check calendar validity; this rule does.

condition:
SELECT wave,
       COUNT(*) AS n_rows,
       COUNT(DISTINCT household_id) AS n_households,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT household_id) AS n_duplicate_household_rows
FROM data
GROUP BY wave
HAVING COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT household_id) > 0
ORDER BY wave
comment: Wave contains more rows than distinct households; per-wave duplicate household extractions listed.

condition: Administrative income or benefit records are attached to this household and must be covered by consent scope and date range.
comment: Linkage-consent reconciliation requires linkage audit metadata that is not part of this dataset. Not executable in QuaC.
